author: @yougeo
text: Have a general question if you don't mind that is we all know carbon fiber is extremely strong and I imagine it's because they've aligned some carbon fibers all in unit directions and then woven them in a pattern that they wish but I'm wondering if it's possible to make a less strong but still strong material but I just using carbon powder in an easily made form such as a carbon foam that could be crushed or other things and then mix with an epoxy and whether that material might be sufficiently strong to be used in a moldable form without having the ultra high strength of carbon fiber which frankly isn't often needed by most projects. What do you think? I know you made some graphene disks that were bulletproof at some point that you got tested but just wondering if there's something that's just super easy to make that could be done obviously not to be bulletproof but just to be a moldable material so you don't have to go to the store to actually buy carbon fiber cloth or fiberglass cloth.

author: @yougeo
text: Many science videos of new physics and things on YouTube but a collection of the ones that I thought were better than the average either in terms of implementation or explanations that seem to have more of a basis in real commonly known physics. So this playlist includes all kinds of things some of which you've already seen in the Stan Myers playlist a couple of videos of your own in there but a lot of interesting subjects that I think you and others might be interested in here and you yourself might be interested in exploring in some of your own future videos. Again as with all things on YouTube stuff is disappearing which is one of the reasons I'm sharing this playlist so hopefully people will download some of these videos and keep them. https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNY9F364UuoMr91tSQSU1M-kZqnOuY6w3

replies.author: @yougeo
replies.text: The friction heater device is an interesting concept where wood is combusted not by open burning by by friction against a spinning disc which seems to much more efficiently convert the higher energy molecule of the wood into the carbon byproducts utilizing steam in a closed system to provide more heat from the wood without losses from a typical fire.

author: @yougeo
text:
More off topic but I. Addition to the Stan Meyers playlist consisting of almost exclusively Meyers himself I compiled a playlist of systems (including some from you) of other people trying to replicate Meyers or do a similar that seemed to be  above the average efforts or explaination. Some detail the bifilar coil which most isn't mention. Some detail other aspects of the circuit usually left out. Some offer easy to find parts for replications. The water spark plug video is interesting.
Unfortunately YouTube's purge of anything non standard continues. These playlists once contains many more videos that are now gone. One in particular separated hydrogen and oxygen in the same cell by using a induced magnetic force in a coil to create a positive versus a negative exit to which because of hydrogen and oxygens differing polarity tended to concentrate the hydrogen at one exit tube versus the oxygen at another which is a very interesting idea.https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNY9F364UuoMrm8UvD1-WI08VMNno8DLU

author: @yougeo
text: Off topic but I wanted to make you aware of the best explanation of the geet reactor that I've ever seen. It actually works in a completely different way than I ever thought with the exhaust product being run back through the pipe with the intake product and some of the exhaust being re fed back into the actual intake which is sort of an interesting and complicated interplay of heat and reactant particles which may actually make it do something interesting possibly. Just thought you might like to see this explanation since I've never seen a good one before.https://youtu.be/d5YsK3bwzPM
